creator: marklmi26-fbsd@yahoo.com
text:
In case it matters, looking in the system llvm sources
indicates that the A78C has both:

FeatureFlagM
FeatureFP16FML

but that X1C does not have those 2. So if FreeBSD
attempts to use either of those based on it looking
at CPU 0's A78C there could be problems when such
code is attempted on a X1C (such a CPU 4).

/usr/main-src/contrib/llvm-project/llvm/lib/Target/AArch64/AArch64.td

has:

  list<SubtargetFeature> A78C = [HasV8_2aOps, FeatureCrypto, FeatureFPARMv8,
                                 FeatureNEON, FeatureFullFP16, FeatureDotProd,
                                 FeatureFlagM, FeatureFP16FML, FeaturePAuth,
                                 FeaturePerfMon, FeatureRCPC, FeatureSPE,
                                 FeatureSSBS];
and:
  list<SubtargetFeature> X1C  = [HasV8_2aOps, FeatureCrypto, FeatureFPARMv8,
                                 FeatureNEON, FeatureRCPC, FeaturePerfMon,
                                 FeatureSPE, FeatureFullFP16, FeatureDotProd,
                                 FeaturePAuth, FeatureSSBS];

(Unfortunately not listed in the same order.)

The A78C is listed as having everything that the
X1C has listed (plus the 2 more).

/usr/main-src/contrib/llvm-project/llvm/include/llvm/Support/AArch64TargetParser.def

agrees about the 2, having (again not in the same order):

AARCH64_CPU_NAME("cortex-a78c", ARMV8_2A, FK_CRYPTO_NEON_FP_ARMV8, false,
                 (AArch64::AEK_FP16 | AArch64::AEK_DOTPROD | AArch64::AEK_RCPC |
                  AArch64::AEK_SSBS | AArch64::AEK_PROFILE | AArch64::AEK_FLAGM |
                  AArch64::AEK_PAUTH | AArch64::AEK_FP16FML))
and:
AARCH64_CPU_NAME("cortex-x1c", ARMV8_2A, FK_CRYPTO_NEON_FP_ARMV8, false,
                 (AArch64::AEK_FP16 | AArch64::AEK_DOTPROD | AArch64::AEK_RCPC |
                  AArch64::AEK_SSBS | AArch64::AEK_PAUTH | AArch64::AEK_PROFILE))

It may well be that FreeBSD makes no use of what
corresponds to FeatureFlagM/AArch64::AEK_FLAGM or
to FeatureFP16FML/AArch64::AEK_FP16FML .
